entities.name: Low-fidelity architecture
entities.context: V's preferred approach: orchestrating 'dumb' mini-models rather than relying on single frontier models for AI system resilience

entities.name: Unix philosophy
entities.context: Design principle referenced as foundation for componentized AI systems—small tools that do one thing well

entities.name: Componentized systems
entities.context: Architectural pattern V believes is more resilient than monolithic systems, reduces blast radius of failures

entities.name: Frontier model
entities.context: Single expensive 'black box' provider approach that V advises against for resilience reasons

entities.name: Orchestration logic
entities.context: Engineering focus V recommends over chasing LLM benchmarks

entities.name: Prompt-chaining
entities.context: Technique for connecting smaller models in V's low-fidelity architecture approach

entities.name: Vendor lock-in
entities.context: Risk that low-fidelity architecture helps avoid by not relying on single provider

entities.name: Blast radius
entities.context: Failure containment concept—componentized systems reduce impact of single model failures

relationships.context: Low-fidelity architecture follows Unix philosophy of small tools doing one thing well
Componentized approach is the foundation of low-fidelity architecture
Both are engineering strategies V recommends for AI system design
